title: DPmixGPD: Model specification and posterior computation
text: bulk model kernel mixture Dirichlet process prior mixing distribution (Ferguson 1973). allocation form, yi∣zi,{Θj}∼k(yi∣Θzi,𝐱),zi∣𝐰∼Categorical(w1,w2,…),   y_i \mid z_i,\{\Theta_j\}\;\sim\;k\!\left(y_i\mid\Theta_{z_i},\boldsymbol{x}_i\right),   \qquad   z_i\mid\boldsymbol{w}\;\sim\;\mathrm{Categorical}(w_1,w_2,\dots), Θj∣H∼iidH,H∼DP(κ,H0),   \Theta_j\mid H\;\overset{\mathrm{iid}}{\sim}\;H,\qquad   H\;\sim\;\mathrm{DP}(\kappa,H_0),  k(⋅∣Θj,𝐱)k(\cdot\mid\Theta_j,\boldsymbol{x}) chosen kernel family (Normal, Gamma, Lognormal, Laplace, Inverse Gaussian, Amoroso, etc.), H0H_0 base measure kernel parameters, κ\kappa controls expected number occupied clusters. Marginally implies (infinite) mixture representation [ f_{}(y) = _{j=1}^{} w_j,k!(y_j,). ]

title: DPmixGPD: Model specification and posterior computation
text: DPmixGPD provides stick-breaking backend (backend="sb") based Sethuraman’s construction (Sethuraman 1994). weights wj=Vj∏ℓ<j(1−Vℓ),Vj∼iidBeta(1,κ),Θj∼iidH0.   w_j = V_j\prod_{\ell<j}(1-V_\ell),   \qquad   V_j\overset{\mathrm{iid}}{\sim}\mathrm{Beta}(1,\kappa),   \qquad   \Theta_j\overset{\mathrm{iid}}{\sim}H_0. computation, package uses finite truncation level JJ (components) works fDPM(y∣𝐱)≈∑j=1Jwjk(y∣Θj,𝐱),   f_{\mathrm{DPM}}(y\mid\boldsymbol{x})\;\approx\;\sum_{j=1}^{J} w_j\,k\!\left(y\mid\Theta_j,\boldsymbol{x}\right),  final weight absorbs leftover mass. standard blocked-Gibbs approximation stick-breaking priors (Ishwaran James 2001). Define counts nj=∑=1N𝕀(zi=j),mj=∑=1N𝕀(zi>j).   n_j = \sum_{=1}^N\mathbb{}(z_i=j),\qquad   m_j = \sum_{=1}^N\mathbb{}(z_i>j).  stick variables conjugate full conditional Vj∣𝐳,κ∼Beta(1+nj,κ+mj),   V_j\mid\boldsymbol{z},\kappa\;\sim\;\mathrm{Beta}(1+n_j,\;\kappa+m_j),  weights reconstructed .

title: DPmixGPD: Model specification and posterior computation
text: DPmixGPD also offers Chinese restaurant process backend (backend="crp"), integrates HH updates allocations induced random partition distribution (blackwell1973?; Neal 2000). view, observation ii joins existing cluster probability proportional current size, starts new cluster probability proportional κ\kappa, coupled base measure H0H_0.

title: DPmixGPD: Model specification and posterior computation
text: Let u(𝐱)u(\boldsymbol{x}) (possibly covariate-dependent) threshold. y>u(𝐱)y>u(\boldsymbol{x}), define exceedance e=y−u(𝐱)e=y-u(\boldsymbol{x}). GPD model Y∣Y>u(𝐱),𝐱∼GPD(u(𝐱),σ(𝐱),ξ),   Y\mid Y>u(\boldsymbol{x}),\boldsymbol{x}\;\sim\;\mathrm{GPD}\big(u(\boldsymbol{x}),\sigma(\boldsymbol{x}),\xi\big),  scale σ(𝐱)>0\sigma(\boldsymbol{x})>0 shape ξ∈ℝ\xi\\mathbb{R}. density fGPD(y∣u(𝐱),σ(𝐱),ξ)={1σ(𝐱)(1+ξy−u(𝐱)σ(𝐱))−1/ξ−1,ξ≠0,1σ(𝐱)exp⁡(−y−u(𝐱)σ(𝐱)),ξ=0, f_{\mathrm{GPD}}\big(y\mid u(\boldsymbol{x}),\sigma(\boldsymbol{x}),\xi\big) = \begin{cases} \dfrac{1}{\sigma(\boldsymbol{x})}\left(1+\xi\dfrac{y-u(\boldsymbol{x})}{\sigma(\boldsymbol{x})}\right)^{-1/\xi-1}, & \xi\neq 0,\\[10pt] \dfrac{1}{\sigma(\boldsymbol{x})}\exp\!\left(-\dfrac{y-u(\boldsymbol{x})}{\sigma(\boldsymbol{x})}\right), & \xi=0, \end{cases}  support constraint 1+ξ{y−u(𝐱)}/σ(𝐱)>01+\xi\{y-u(\boldsymbol{x})\}/\sigma(\boldsymbol{x})>0. CDF FGPD(y∣u(𝐱),σ(𝐱),ξ)={1−(1+ξy−u(𝐱)σ(𝐱))−1/ξ,ξ≠0,1−exp⁡(−y−u(𝐱)σ(𝐱)),ξ=0. F_{\mathrm{GPD}}\big(y\mid u(\boldsymbol{x}),\sigma(\boldsymbol{x}),\xi\big) = \begin{cases} 1-\left(1+\xi\dfrac{y-u(\boldsymbol{x})}{\sigma(\boldsymbol{x})}\right)^{-1/\xi}, & \xi\neq 0,\\[10pt] 1-\exp\!\left(-\dfrac{y-u(\boldsymbol{x})}{\sigma(\boldsymbol{x})}\right), & \xi=0. \end{cases} use GPD exceedances justified classical peaks--threshold limit theory (Balkema Haan 1974; Pickands 1975; Coles 2001).

title: DPmixGPD: Model specification and posterior computation
text: DPmixGPD defines single proper density splicing bulk DPM GPD tail u(𝐱)u(\boldsymbol{x}): f(y∣𝐱)={fDPM(y∣𝐱),y≤u(𝐱),(1−FDPM(u(𝐱)∣𝐱))fGPD(y∣u(𝐱),σ(𝐱),ξ),y>u(𝐱). f(y\mid\boldsymbol{x}) = \begin{cases} f_{\mathrm{DPM}}(y\mid\boldsymbol{x}), & y\le u(\boldsymbol{x}),\\[6pt] \Big(1-F_{\mathrm{DPM}}\big(u(\boldsymbol{x})\mid\boldsymbol{x}\big)\Big)\,f_{\mathrm{GPD}}\big(y\mid u(\boldsymbol{x}),\sigma(\boldsymbol{x}),\xi\big), & y>u(\boldsymbol{x}). \end{cases}  multiplicative factor 1−FDPM{u(𝐱)∣𝐱}1-F_{\mathrm{DPM}}\{u(\boldsymbol{x})\mid\boldsymbol{x}\} ensures total tail probability mass matches bulk survival beyond threshold. Equivalently, spliced CDF F(y∣𝐱)={FDPM(y∣𝐱),y≤u(𝐱),FDPM(u(𝐱)∣𝐱)+(1−FDPM(u(𝐱)∣𝐱))FGPD(y∣u(𝐱),σ(𝐱),ξ),y>u(𝐱). F(y\mid\boldsymbol{x}) = \begin{cases} F_{\mathrm{DPM}}(y\mid\boldsymbol{x}), & y\le u(\boldsymbol{x}),\\[6pt] F_{\mathrm{DPM}}\big(u(\boldsymbol{x})\mid\boldsymbol{x}\big) +\Big(1-F_{\mathrm{DPM}}\big(u(\boldsymbol{x})\mid\boldsymbol{x}\big)\Big) \,F_{\mathrm{GPD}}\big(y\mid u(\boldsymbol{x}),\sigma(\boldsymbol{x}),\xi\big), & y>u(\boldsymbol{x}). \end{cases} section reference point later predictive quantities (density, survival, quantiles, exceedance probabilities).

text: spliced model, observation contributes either bulk likelihood term tail likelihood term, depending whether falls threshold. fixed threshold uu (covariates), full likelihood ℒ(Θ,𝐰,σ,ξ∣𝐲)=∏:yi≤ufDPM(yi)×∏:yi>u(1−FDPM(u))fGPD(yi∣u,σ,ξ).   \mathcal{L}(\Theta,\boldsymbol{w},\sigma,\xi\mid\boldsymbol{y})   =   \prod_{: y_i\le u} f_{\mathrm{DPM}}(y_i)   \times   \prod_{: y_i>u} \Big(1-F_{\mathrm{DPM}}(u)\Big)\,f_{\mathrm{GPD}}(y_i\mid u,\sigma,\xi).  covariates, replace uu u(𝐱)u(\boldsymbol{x}_i) use fDPM(⋅∣𝐱)f_{\mathrm{DPM}}(\cdot\mid\boldsymbol{x}_i) FDPM(⋅∣𝐱)F_{\mathrm{DPM}}(\cdot\mid\boldsymbol{x}_i).

title: DPmixGPD: Model specification and posterior computation
text: DPmixGPD targets joint posterior mixture allocations, kernel parameters, weights, (optionally) tail parameters. stick-breaking backend, typical blocked-Gibbs iteration updates: Allocation indicators ziz_i via categorical probabilities proportional wjk(yi∣Θj,𝐱)w_j\,k(y_i\mid\Theta_j,\boldsymbol{x}_i), splicing handled tail module yi>u(𝐱)y_i>u(\boldsymbol{x}_i). Stick variables VjV_j (hence wjw_j) conjugate beta full conditionals. Kernel parameters Θj\Theta_j full conditional distributions. conjugacy unavailable, package uses Metropolis–Hastings steps inside Gibbs loop. Tail parameters (u(⋅),σ(⋅),ξ)(u(\cdot),\sigma(\cdot),\xi) according user-specified param_specs. Fixed parameters held constant; prior-based parameters updated conjugate Metropolis steps depending chosen prior family. concentration parameter κ\kappa assigned prior (typically gamma prior), via conjugate Metropolis updates. CRP backend updates allocations partition distribution updates cluster parameters accordingly.

title: DPmixGPD: Model specification and posterior computation
text: Posterior prediction implemented S3 method predict() mixgpd_fit objects. functional g(θ)g(\theta) model parameters, prediction follows standard Bayesian recipe: p(ỹ∣𝐱,𝒟)=∫p(ỹ∣𝐱,θ)p(θ∣𝒟)dθ,   p(\tilde{y}\mid\boldsymbol{x},\mathcal{D}) = \int p(\tilde{y}\mid\boldsymbol{x},\theta)\,p(\theta\mid\mathcal{D})\,d\theta,  approximated Monte Carlo posterior draws θ(s)\theta^{(s)}. particular, density: f(ỹ∣𝐱,𝒟)≈S−1∑s=1Sf(ỹ∣𝐱,θ(s))f(\tilde{y}\mid\boldsymbol{x},\mathcal{D}) \approx S^{-1}\sum_{s=1}^S f(\tilde{y}\mid\boldsymbol{x},\theta^{(s)}), survival: S(ỹ∣𝐱,𝒟)=1−F(ỹ∣𝐱,𝒟)S(\tilde{y}\mid\boldsymbol{x},\mathcal{D}) = 1-F(\tilde{y}\mid\boldsymbol{x},\mathcal{D}), quantiles: Q(p∣𝐱,𝒟)Q(p\mid\boldsymbol{x},\mathcal{D}) obtained inverting posterior predictive CDF draw--draw summarizing across draws.

title: DPmixGPD: Model fundamentals (bulk DPM + GPD tail)
text: Let y1,…,yny_1,\dots,y_n denote outcomes. DPmixGPD uses mixture representation yi∣zi=j∼k(yi∣Θj),zi∼Categorical(w1,w2,…),   y_i \mid z_i=j \sim k(y_i \mid \Theta_j), \qquad   z_i \sim \mathrm{Categorical}(w_1,w_2,\dots),  bulk density fDPM(y)=∑j=1∞wjk(y∣Θj).   f_{\mathrm{DPM}}(y)   =   \sum_{j=1}^\infty w_j \, k(y \mid \Theta_j). Dirichlet process prior imposed random mixing measure GG underlying parameters Θj\Theta_j: G∼DP(α,G0),Θj∼iidG.   G \sim \mathrm{DP}(\alpha, G_0), \qquad \Theta_j \overset{iid}{\sim} G.  α>0\alpha>0 concentration parameter controlling expected clustering, G0G_0 base measure encoding prior beliefs kernel parameters (Ferguson 1973). DPmixGPD supports two equivalent DP representations: stick-breaking (“SB”) (Sethuraman 1994), implemented finite truncation JJ components (Ishwaran James 2001); Chinese restaurant process (“CRP”), partition-based formulation (blackwell1973?; Neal 2000). SB form (truncated JJ), vj∼Beta(1,α),w1=v1,wj=vj∏ℓ<j(1−vℓ)(j≥2),wJ=1−∑j=1J−1wj.   v_j \sim \mathrm{Beta}(1,\alpha),\quad   w_1=v_1,\quad   w_j=v_j\prod_{\ell<j}(1-v_\ell)\ (j\ge 2),\quad   w_J = 1-\sum_{j=1}^{J-1} w_j.

title: DPmixGPD: Model fundamentals (bulk DPM + GPD tail)
text: upper-tail modeling, fix threshold uu define exceedances x=y−u>0x=y-u>0 y>uy>u. GPD density exceedances g(x∣σ,ξ)=1σ(1+ξxσ)−1/ξ−1,x>0,σ>0,   g(x\mid \sigma,\xi)   =   \frac{1}{\sigma}   \left(1+\xi\frac{x}{\sigma}\right)^{-1/\xi - 1},   \qquad x>0,\ \sigma>0,  support constraint 1+ξx/σ>01+\xi x/\sigma>0. corresponding CDF G(x∣σ,ξ)=1−(1+ξxσ)−1/ξ(ξ≠0),   G(x\mid\sigma,\xi)   =   1-\left(1+\xi\frac{x}{\sigma}\right)^{-1/\xi}   \quad (\xi\ne 0),  limit ξ→0\xi\0, G(x∣σ,0)=1−exp⁡(−x/σ).   G(x\mid\sigma,0)=1-\exp(-x/\sigma).  GPD limit result threshold exceedances central extreme value theory (Balkema Haan 1974; Pickands 1975; Coles 2001).

title: DPmixGPD: Model fundamentals (bulk DPM + GPD tail)
text: DPmixGPD combines bulk model FDPMF_{\mathrm{DPM}} GPD exceedance model ensuring resulting full distribution integrates 1. Let uu denote tail threshold. Define: pu=FDPM(u),p‾u=1−pu.   p_u = F_{\mathrm{DPM}}(u), \qquad \bar p_u = 1 - p_u.  spliced CDF defined : F(y)={FDPM(y),y≤u,pu+p‾uG(y−u∣σ,ξ),y>u. F(y) = \begin{cases} F_{\mathrm{DPM}}(y), & y \le u,\\[6pt] p_u + \bar p_u \, G(y-u\mid\sigma,\xi), & y>u. \end{cases}  construction, FF nondecreasing, right-continuous, satisfies lim⁡y→−∞F(y)=0\lim_{y\-\infty}F(y)=0, lim⁡y→∞F(y)=1\lim_{y\\infty}F(y)=1. corresponding density : f(y)={fDPM(y),y≤u,p‾ug(y−u∣σ,ξ),y>u. f(y) = \begin{cases} f_{\mathrm{DPM}}(y), & y \le u,\\[6pt] \bar p_u \, g(y-u\mid\sigma,\xi), & y>u. \end{cases}  “bulk + tail using observations” Bayesian philosophy matches earlier semiparametric bulk–tail constructions (Frigessi et al. 2002; Behrens et al. 2004; Nascimento et al. 2012).

title: DPmixGPD: Model fundamentals (bulk DPM + GPD tail)
text: DPmixGPD implements models NIMBLE-backed MCMC. high level, posterior inference alternates : allocation updates ziz_i (component membership), component parameter updates Θj\Theta_j, mixture weight updates (SB/CRP), tail updates (u,σ,ξ)(u,\sigma,\xi) GPD = TRUE, hyperparameter updates (e.g., α\alpha) stochastic. user-facing workflows produce mixgpd_fit object (causal fit object wrapping two mixgpd_fits), supports predict(), fitted(), related S3 methods.

what: Amoroso mixture with a GPD tail — amoroso_mixgpd
title: Amoroso mixture with a GPD tail — amoroso_mixgpd
text: family splices generalized Pareto distribution (GPD) threshold threshold onto Amoroso mixture bulk. Let \(F_{mix}\) denote Amoroso mixture CDF. spliced CDF \(F(x)=F_{mix}(x)\) \(x<threshold\) \(F(x)=F_{mix}(threshold) + \left\{1-F_{mix}(threshold)\right\}G(x)\) \(x\ge threshold\), \(G\) GPD CDF exceedances threshold.

title: Predict from a MixGPD fit — predict.mixgpd_fit
text: object stores dedicated predictive machinery, can keep signature swap internals without breaking user code. type="mean" option computes posterior predictive mean : posterior draw s, computing E(Y | x, theta_s) via Monte Carlo simulation Averaging conditional means posterior: E(Y | x, data) = mean_s(E(Y | x, theta_s)) accounts parameter uncertainty Bayesian predictive mean. tail shape parameter xi >= 1 (heavy tail), mean undefined function returns Inf warning suggesting alternatives like median restricted mean.

title: Predict from a MixGPD fit — predict.mixgpd_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ y <- abs(stats::rnorm(50)) + 0.1 bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(y = y, backend = "sb", kernel = "normal",                              GPD = TRUE, components = 6,                              mcmc = list(niter = 200, nburnin = 50, thin = 1, nchains = 1)) fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle) pr <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", p = c(0.5, 0.9)) pr_surv <- predict(fit, y = sort(y), type = "survival") pr_cdf <- list(fit = 1 - pr_surv$fit) # HPD intervals pr_hpd <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", p = c(0.5, 0.9), interval = "hpd") # No intervals pr_none <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", p = c(0.5, 0.9), interval = NULL) # Restricted mean (finite under heavy tails) pr_rmean <- predict(fit, type = "rmean", cutoff = 10, interval = "credible") } # }

"bayes_mean": Bayesian PIT using draw-wise CDFs averaged draws. "bayes_draw": Bayesian PIT using single draw-wise CDF per observation.
